{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e0336afa9eef41b4be430dd8","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tZAI Response Parser - Centralized utility for parsing LLM responses.\n3\t\n4\tHandles:\n5\t1. ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n6\t2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n7\t3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n8\t4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n9\t\n10\tThis utility is used across:\n11\t- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n12\t- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n13\t- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n14\t- Any other LLM interaction points\n15\t\n16\t## Error Handling Patterns\n17\t\n18\tWhen ParseLLMError is raised, choose recovery strategy based on context:\n19\t\n20\t### Corrective Retry Pattern (Router-style)\n21\tUse when:\n22\t- LLM call is early in pipeline (cheap to retry)\n23\t- Failure cascades to downstream operations\n24\t- Retrying doesn't require re-running expensive operations\n25\t\n26\tExample: Intent router retries once because malformed response affects all downstream processing.\n27\t\n28\tSee: src/intent/router.py classify_utterance()\n29\t\n30\t### Fallback Result Pattern (Synthesize-style)\n31\tUse when:\n32\t- LLM call is AFTER expensive operations (fetch, DB queries, etc.)\n33\t- Data already obtained should not be discarded\n34\t- User should see partial results in degraded-state UX\n35\t\n36\tExample: Synthesize strand returns fallback result because fetch operations already completed.\n37\t\n38\tSee: src/synthesize/strand.py synthesize()\n39\t\n40\t### Documentation\n41\tSee: docs/error-handling-standardization.md for complete pattern comparison and examples.\n42\t\"\"\"\n43\t\n44\timport json\n45\tfrom logging import getLogger\n46\tfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Union, cast\n47\t\n48\tlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n49\t\n50\t\n51\tclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n52\t    \"\"\"\n53\t    Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\n54\t\n55\t    Attributes:\n56\t        message: Human-readable error message\n57\t        raw_response: The raw LLM response text that failed parsing (preserved for debugging)\n58\t\n59\t    The raw_response attribute is critical for debugging and degraded-state UX.\n60\t    Always pass it when raising ParseLLMError so error handlers can include\n61\t    response snippets in error events and logs.\n62\t    \"\"\"\n63\t\n64\t    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n65\t        self.raw_response = raw_response\n66\t        super().__init__(message)\n67\t\n68\t\n69\tdef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n70\t    \"\"\"\n71\t    Strip markdown code fences from a response using optimized position-based parsing.\n72\t\n73\t    Handles:\n74\t    - ```json ... ```\n75\t    - ``` ... ```\n76\t    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n77\t\n78\t    Performance: Uses find()/rfind() for single-pass fence removal, avoiding intermediate\n79\t    string allocations from split()/rsplit(). This is 10-15% faster than split-based\n80\t    approaches (see benchmarking in notes/adc-3e5gg.md). This is the optimal approach\n81\t    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n82\t\n83\t    Args:\n84\t        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n85\t\n86\t    Returns:\n87\t        Text with markdown fences removed\n88\t\n89\t    Examples:\n90\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n91\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n92\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n93\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n94\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n95\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n96\t    \"\"\"\n97\t    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n98\t    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n99\t        return raw\n100\t\n101\t    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n102\t    text = raw.strip()\n103\t\n104\t    # Fast manual fence stripping using position-based search\n105\t    # Pattern: ```optional_lang\\n content \\n```\n106\t    # Uses find()/rfind() instead of split()/rsplit() to avoid intermediate allocations\n107\t    if text.startswith(\"```\"):\n108\t        # Find first newline after opening fence (position-based, no split)\n109\t        nl_pos = text.find(\"\\n\")\n110\t        # Find closing fence from end (search backwards for last ```)\n111\t        fence_end = text.rfind(\"```\")\n112\t\n113\t        # Direct slice extraction with single strip\n114\t        if nl_pos != -1 and fence_end > nl_pos:\n115\t            text = text[nl_pos + 1:fence_end].strip()\n116\t\n117\t    return text\n118\t\n119\t\n120\tdef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n121\t    \"\"\"\n122\t    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n123\t\n124\t    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n125\t    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n126\t\n127\t    Args:\n128\t        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n129\t\n130\t    Returns:\n131\t        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n132\t\n133\t    Examples:\n134\t        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n135\t        {\"content\": [...]}\n136\t        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n137\t        {\"content\": [...]}\n138\t    \"\"\"\n139\t    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n140\t    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n141\t        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n142\t        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n143\t        return inner\n144\t\n145\t    return response_data\n146\t\n147\t\n148\tdef extract_text_from_response(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n149\t    \"\"\"\n150\t    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n151\t\n152\t    Args:\n153\t        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n154\t\n155\t    Returns:\n156\t        Extracted text content\n157\t\n158\t    Raises:\n159\t        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n160\t    \"\"\"\n161\t    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n162\t\n163\t    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n164\t        text = cast(str, content[0].get(\"text\", \"\"))\n165\t        if text:\n166\t            return text\n167\t\n168\t    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n169\t    logger.warning(\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n170\t    return str(content)\n171\t\n172\t\n173\tdef parse_llm_response(\n174\t    raw_text: str,\n175\t    *,\n176\t    strip_fences: bool = True,\n177\t    expect_json: bool = True,\n178\t) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n179\t    \"\"\"\n180\t    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n181\t\n182\t    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n183\t    It handles the common pattern of:\n184\t    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n185\t    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n186\t    3. Providing clear error messages\n187\t\n188\t    Performance: Optimized with fast fence stripping and minimal overhead.\n189\t    Uses strip_markdown_fences() for fence removal which is 7-179x faster than regex.\n190\t\n191\t    Args:\n192\t        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n193\t        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n194\t        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n195\t\n196\t    Returns:\n197\t        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n198\t\n199\t    Raises:\n200\t        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n201\t\n202\t    Examples:\n203\t        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n204\t        {'a': 1}\n205\t        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n206\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n207\t    \"\"\"\n208\t    try:\n209\t        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present (reuses optimized function)\n210\t        if strip_fences:\n211\t            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n212\t        else:\n213\t            text = raw_text.strip() if raw_text else \"\"\n214\t\n215\t        # Step 2: Check for empty input (after fence stripping)\n216\t        if not text:\n217\t            if expect_json:\n218\t                raise ParseLLMError(\n219\t                    \"Empty response provided\",\n220\t                    raw_response=raw_text,\n221\t                )\n222\t            return raw_text\n223\t\n224\t        # Step 3: Parse JSON if expected\n225\t        if expect_json:\n226\t            try:\n227\t                return json.loads(text)\n228\t            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n229\t                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n230\t                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n231\t                raise ParseLLMError(\n232\t                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n233\t                    raw_response=raw_text,\n234\t                ) from e\n235\t\n236\t        return text\n237\t\n238\t    except ParseLLMError:\n239\t        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n240\t        raise\n241\t    except Exception as e:\n242\t        raise ParseLLMError(\n243\t            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n244\t            raw_response=raw_text,\n245\t        ) from e\n246\t\n247\t\n248\tdef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n249\t    response_dict: Dict[str, Any],\n250\t    *,\n251\t    extract_text: bool = True,\n252\t    strip_fences: bool = True,\n253\t    expect_json: bool = True,\n254\t) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n255\t    \"\"\"\n256\t    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n257\t\n258\t    This handles the full flow:\n259\t    1. Unwrap ZAI proxy's \"result\" field\n260\t    2. Extract text from Anthropic content array\n261\t    3. Strip markdown fences\n262\t    4. Parse JSON if expected\n263\t\n264\t    Args:\n265\t        response_dict: Raw response dict from httpx.Response.json()\n266\t        extract_text: Whether to extract text from content array (default: True)\n267\t        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown fences (default: True)\n268\t        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n269\t\n270\t    Returns:\n271\t        Parsed JSON object or text depending on expect_json\n272\t\n273\t    Raises:\n274\t        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n275\t\n276\t    Examples:\n277\t        >>> response = {\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": '```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```'}]}}\n278\t        >>> parse_zai_proxy_response(response)\n279\t        {'a': 1}\n280\t    \"\"\"\n281\t    try:\n282\t        # Step 1: Unwrap ZAI proxy response\n283\t        unwrapped = unwrap_zai_response(response_dict)\n284\t\n285\t        # Step 2: Extract text from content structure\n286\t        if extract_text:\n287\t            text = extract_text_from_response(unwrapped)\n288\t        else:\n289\t            # Return the unwrapped payload as-is\n290\t            return unwrapped\n291\t\n292\t        # Step 3 & 4: Strip fences and parse JSON using the main parser\n293\t        return parse_llm_response(\n294\t            text,\n295\t            strip_fences=strip_fences,\n296\t            expect_json=expect_json,\n297\t        )\n298\t\n299\t    except ParseLLMError:\n300\t        raise\n301\t    except Exception as e:\n302\t        raise ParseLLMError(\n303\t            f\"Failed to parse ZAI proxy response: {e}\",\n304\t            raw_response=str(response_dict)[:500],\n305\t        ) from e\n306\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0a05d9c4-3cab-426c-a5a2-b21c4918fda8","uuid":"00dfd759-9393-4306-8c6c-6888c67bb6f1","timestamp":"2026-07-24T04:34:49.758Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/llm/response_parser.py","content":"\"\"\"\nZAI Response Parser - Centralized utility for parsing LLM responses.\n\nHandles:\n1. ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n\nThis utility is used across:\n- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n- Any other LLM interaction points\n\n## Error Handling Patterns\n\nWhen ParseLLMError is raised, choose recovery strategy based on context:\n\n### Corrective Retry Pattern (Router-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is early in pipeline (cheap to retry)\n- Failure cascades to downstream operations\n- Retrying doesn't require re-running expensive operations\n\nExample: Intent router retries once because malformed response affects all downstream processing.\n\nSee: src/intent/router.py classify_utterance()\n\n### Fallback Result Pattern (Synthesize-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is AFTER expensive operations (fetch, DB queries, etc.)\n- Data already obtained should not be discarded\n- User should see partial results in degraded-state UX\n\nExample: Synthesize strand returns fallback result because fetch operations already completed.\n\nSee: src/synthesize/strand.py synthesize()\n\n### Documentation\nSee: docs/error-handling-standardization.md for complete pattern comparison and examples.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Union, cast\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"\n    Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\n\n    Attributes:\n        message: Human-readable error message\n        raw_response: The raw LLM response text that failed parsing (preserved for debugging)\n\n    The raw_response attribute is critical for debugging and degraded-state UX.\n    Always pass it when raising ParseLLMError so error handlers can include\n    response snippets in error events and logs.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.raw_response = raw_response\n        super().__init__(message)\n\n\ndef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Strip markdown code fences from a response using optimized position-based parsing.\n\n    Handles:\n    - ```json ... ```\n    - ``` ... ```\n    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n\n    Performance: Uses find()/rfind() for single-pass fence removal, avoiding intermediate\n    string allocations from split()/rsplit(). This is 10-15% faster than split-based\n    approaches (see benchmarking in notes/adc-3e5gg.md). This is the optimal approach\n    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n\n    Args:\n        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n\n    Returns:\n        Text with markdown fences removed\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n        return raw\n\n    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n    text = raw.strip()\n\n    # Fast manual fence stripping using position-based search\n    # Pattern: ```optional_lang\\n content \\n```\n    # Uses find()/rfind() instead of split()/rsplit() to avoid intermediate allocations\n    if text.startswith(\"```\"):\n        # Find first newline after opening fence (position-based, no split)\n        nl_pos = text.find(\"\\n\")\n        # Find closing fence from end (search backwards for last ```)\n        fence_end = text.rfind(\"```\")\n\n        # Direct slice extraction with single strip\n        if nl_pos != -1 and fence_end > nl_pos:\n            text = text[nl_pos + 1:fence_end].strip()\n\n    return text\n\n\ndef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n\n    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n\n    Args:\n        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n\n    Returns:\n        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n        return inner\n\n    return response_data\n\n\ndef extract_text_from_response(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n\n    Args:\n        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n\n    Returns:\n        Extracted text content\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n    \"\"\"\n    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n\n    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n        text = cast(str, content[0].get(\"text\", \"\"))\n        if text:\n            return text\n\n    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n    logger.warning(\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n    return str(content)\n\n\ndef parse_llm_response(\n    raw_text: str,\n    *,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n\n    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n    It handles the common pattern of:\n    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n    3. Providing clear error messages\n\n    Performance: Optimized with fast fence stripping and minimal overhead.\n    Uses strip_markdown_fences() for fence removal which is 7-179x faster than regex.\n\n    Args:\n        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        {'a': 1}\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present (reuses optimized function)\n        if strip_fences:\n            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n        else:\n            text = raw_text.strip() if raw_text else \"\"\n\n        # Step 2: Check for empty input (after fence stripping)\n        if not text:\n            if expect_json:\n                raise ParseLLMError(\n                    \"Empty response provided\",\n                    raw_response=raw_text,\n                )\n            return raw_text\n\n        # Step 3: Parse JSON if expected\n        if expect_json:\n            try:\n                return json.loads(text)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n                raise ParseLLMError(\n                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n                    raw_response=raw_text,\n                ) from e\n\n        return text\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=raw_text,\n        ) from e\n\n\ndef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n    response_dict: Dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    extract_text: bool = True,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n\n    This handles the full flow:\n    1. Unwrap ZAI proxy's \"result\" field\n    2. Extract text from Anthropic content array\n    3. Strip markdown fences\n    4. Parse JSON if expected\n\n    Args:\n        response_dict: Raw response dict from httpx.Response.json()\n        extract_text: Whether to extract text from content array (default: True)\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object or text depending on expect_json\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> response = {\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": '```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```'}]}}\n        >>> parse_zai_proxy_response(response)\n        {'a': 1}\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Unwrap ZAI proxy response\n        unwrapped = unwrap_zai_response(response_dict)\n\n        # Step 2: Extract text from content structure\n        if extract_text:\n            text = extract_text_from_response(unwrapped)\n        else:\n            # Return the unwrapped payload as-is\n            return unwrapped\n\n        # Step 3 & 4: Strip fences and parse JSON using the main parser\n        return parse_llm_response(\n            text,\n            strip_fences=strip_fences,\n            expect_json=expect_json,\n        )\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Failed to parse ZAI proxy response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=str(response_dict)[:500],\n        ) from e\n","numLines":306,"startLine":1,"totalLines":306}}}

parsing from src/intent/router.py.\n\n    This handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced JSON responses with minimal overhead.\n    Uses find()/rfind() instead of split() to avoid intermediate string strip_markdown_fences,\n    parse_llm_response,\n    ParseLLMError,\n)\n\n\ndef parse_router_style(response):\n    \"\"\"\n    Replicate the optimized JSON parsing from src/intent/router.py.\n\n    This handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced JSON responses with minimal overhead.\n    Uses find()/rfind() instead of split() to avoid intermediate string allocations.\n    \"\"\"\n    raw = response.strip()\n\n    # Fast path for fenced responses (GLM-4.7 default)\n    if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n        # Find first newline after opening fence (position-based, no split)\n        nl_pos = raw.find(\"\\n\")\n        # Find closing fence from end (search backwards for last ```)\n        fence_end = raw.rfind(\"```\")\n\n        # Direct slice extraction with single strip\n        if nl_pos != -1 and fence_end > nl_pos:\n            raw = raw[nl_pos + 1:fence_end].strip()\n\n    return json.loads(raw)\n\n\nclass TestJSONParsing:\n    \"\"\"Test suite for optimized JSON parsing.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_fenced_json_with_language_specifier(self):\n        \"\"\"Test parsing fenced JSON with language specifier (```json).\"\"\"\n        response = '''```json\n[\n  {\n    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n    \"project_slug\": \"aide-de-camp\",\n    \"confidence\": 0.9\n  }\n]\n```'''\n        result = parse_router_style(response)\n        assert isinstance(result, list)\n = raw.find(\"\\n\")\n        # Find closing fence from end (search backwards for last ```)\n        fence_end = raw.rfind(\"```\")\n\n        # Direct slice extraction with single strip\n        if This handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced JSON responses with minimal overhead.\n    Uses find()/rfind() instead of split() to avoid intermediate string allocations.\n    \"\"\"\n    raw = response.strip()\n\n    # Fast path for fenced responses (GLM-4.7 default)\n    if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n        # Find first newline after opening fence (position-based, no split)\n        nl_pos = raw.find(\"\\n\")\n        # Find closing fence from end (search backwards for last ```)\n        fence_end = raw.rfind(\"```\")\n\n        # Direct slice extraction with single strip\n        if nl_pos != -1 and fence_end > nl_pos:\n            raw = raw[nl_pos + 1:fence_end].strip()\n\n    return json.loads(raw)\n\n\nclass TestJSONParsing:\n    \"\"\"Test suite for optimized JSON
